summary: All three attempts failed to resolve two codeql:py/path-injection findings because sanitize_filename() only strips non-alphanumeric characters but does not validate that the resulting path stays within CONFIG_DIR. The code lacks confinement checks using os.path.realpath or os.path.abspath, leaving both os.path.exists() and open() on user-controlled paths.
suggested_actions: After constructing filepath via os.path.join(CONFIG_DIR, filename), call os.path.realpath on both the result and CONFIG_DIR, then verify the real path starts with the real CONFIG_DIR prefix.
Alternatively, use os.path.abspath and os.path.commonpath to ensure the resolved path is within the allowed directory.
Reject filenames that contain '..' or symbolic link components even after sanitization.
